Think about that: In the entire history of this country, no woman scientist has ever had a vote in our nation's legislature. That is not acceptable. It is time to change that. To do it, I need your help. Republicans would rather elect extremist yes-men who are backed by corporate PACs and far-right groups -- they're terrified of a woman scientist holding them accountable. But I'm powered by grassroots support -- people like you who believe facts still matter. [**With Election Day coming up on Tuesday, I need your help to get every last voter to the polls. name: Kristen McDonald Rivet
subject: What’s on your mind, friend?
body:
|
#
Friend, I know how busy this time of the year can be, so I wanted to follow up on the issues survey I sent earlier this week. I want to know what’s on your mind – because what matters most to you, matters to me too. That’s why it’s so important for me to hear directly from you: [**If you have a minute to spare, I’d love to hear from you. Will you take the survey today?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/131579142/621593391/-1183440506?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NjAyNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxMDExYjJmZi0xMzUyLWYxMTEtOGVmMi0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=sEMrr1arwO9b_2w-RKaez1VTc_RfinxgfdbbSHPAETU=&emci=b1069eaa-7150-f111-8ef2-000d3a14b640&emdi=1011b2ff-1352-f111-8ef2-000d3a14b640&ceid=27257697) | [Take the survey](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/131579143/621593392/-1183440506?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NjAyNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxMDExYjJmZi0xMzUyLWYxMTEtOGVmMi0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=sEMrr1arwO9b_2w-RKaez1VTc_RfinxgfdbbSHPAETU=&emci=b1069eaa-7150-f111-8ef2-000d3a14b640&emdi=1011b2ff-1352-f111-8ef2-000d3a14b640&ceid=27257697)
---
We know the system isn’t working, and that’s why I’m here to take on the problems Washington has ignored. We’ve already made a strong start by fighting to improve access to health care, protect Social Security, and lower the cost of living for mid-Michiganders – but our work is just beginning.
**That’s why I _need_ to hear from folks like you. Your input is invaluable, and it will help shape the priorities this campaign takes on next.**
[**I’d really appreciate it if you could take a moment and share your thoughts with us.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/131579144/621593393/-1183440506?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NjAyNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxMDExYjJmZi0xMzUyLWYxMTEtOGVmMi0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=sEMrr1arwO9b_2w-RKaez1VTc_RfinxgfdbbSHPAETU=&emci=b1069eaa-7150-f111-8ef2-000d3a14b640&emdi=1011b2ff-1352-f111-8ef2-000d3a14b640&ceid=27257697)
[Take the survey](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/131579145/621593394/-1183440506?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NjAyNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxMDExYjJmZi0xMzUyLWYxMTEtOGVmMi0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=sEMrr1arwO9b_2w-RKaez1VTc_RfinxgfdbbSHPAETU=&emci=b1069eaa-7150-f111-8ef2-000d3a14b640&emdi=1011b2ff-1352-f111-8ef2-000d3a14b640&ceid=27257697)
---
Thank you,
Kristen

**\------ Forwarded Message ------**
From: Kristen McDonald Rivet
Date: Tue, May 12, 2026 at 4:16 PM
Subject: I want to hear from you directly, friend.
Friend,
This first year in Congress has really reinforced something for me: people are just plain tired.
Tired of the division. Tired of the political games. And tired of working hard while it feels like everything just keeps getting more expensive.
My team and I have spent the last year all over mid-Michigan – in union halls, family farms, church basements, and living rooms – and no matter where we go, we hear the same thing:
**People want leaders who will actually do something.**
And look, Washington can be very frustrating. But I still believe bringing people together to get things done matters.
It’s how we stopped Elon Musk from closing the Saginaw Social Security office.
It’s how we protected funding for the Flint Registry when MAGA Republicans tried to cut it.
And it’s how we passed my bipartisan hot rotisserie chicken bill out of the House, which is going to help SNAP families take home a hot, ready-to-go meal.
That’s the work I came here to do: lower costs, take on corruption, protect health care, and fight for working families. But there’s still a lot more to do, and before this campaign really picks up, I want to hear from you.
[**That’s why I’m asking: Will you take a minute to fill out my quick issues survey?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/131579146/621593395/-1408965237?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NjAyNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxMDExYjJmZi0xMzUyLWYxMTEtOGVmMi0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=sEMrr1arwO9b_2w-RKaez1VTc_RfinxgfdbbSHPAETU=&emci=b1069eaa-7150-f111-8ef2-000d3a14b640&emdi=1011b2ff-1352-f111-8ef2-000d3a14b640&ceid=27257697)
[Take the survey](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/131579147/621593396/-1183440506?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NjAyNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxMDExYjJmZi0xMzUyLWYxMTEtOGVmMi0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=sEMrr1arwO9b_2w-RKaez1VTc_RfinxgfdbbSHPAETU=&emci=b1069eaa-7150-f111-8ef2-000d3a14b640&emdi=1011b2ff-1352-f111-8ef2-000d3a14b640&ceid=27257697)
---
I look forward to hearing from you, friend.
Kristen

name: David Hogg
subject: They’re counting on our exhaustion
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE
body:
The crises Trump is manufacturing aren’t slowing down, and neither can our work.
# [](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/852?refcode=em20260517-1&t=1&akid=3490%2E587540%2E0mUDiv)
**Recurring donors help me sleep at night, Peter.**
And right now, I could use a solid night of rest.
April and May are typically lower fundraising months. It’s the height of tax season, families are planning their summer vacations, and honestly, people are tired. November starts to feel like a long way away.
I get it. Life happens, and it’s hard to stay focused when the news cycle never stops. Trump knows that — actually, _he counts on it._
His policies are deeply unpopular, his approval ratings are underwater, and his MAGA base is imploding. Instead of changing what he’s doing, **he manufactures constant chaos and distractions, designed to leave people feeling hopeless and keep them from building momentum.**
But regardless of what the headlines say, the problems we’re facing aren’t going away. The attacks on our rights, working families being squeezed dry at every turn, corporations buying up influence in our elections — they don’t stop because it’s a slow fundraising month. Neither can our work.
Here’s the honest truth: getting through this period requires stability. That stability comes from our recurring donors. When we can count on recurring donors to show up every single month, it makes it possible for us to plan ahead, invest early, and fight back consistently.
We don’t take money from corporate PACs or special interests. We depend on our grassroots community showing up for us because they truly believe in what we’re building at Leaders We Deserve. And right now, in this slow period, your support means more than ever.
**[If you’re able, can you power our work with a monthly contribution through Election Day? Even $5 or $10 a month gives us the stability we need to keep our candidates in the fight, no matter what distractions get thrown our way.](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/5229?c0=em_ms_lwd_20260517_1_sleep.5daazi&refcode=em20260517-3490&amount=5&t=2&akid=3490%2E587540%2E0mUDiv)**

clean_body: The crises Trump is manufacturing aren’t slowing down, and neither can our work. Recurring donors help me sleep at night, Peter. And right now, I could use a solid night of rest. April and May are typically lower fundraising months. It’s the height of tax season, families are planning their summer vacations, and honestly, people are tired. November starts to feel like a long way away. I get it. Life happens, and it’s hard to stay focused when the news cycle never stops. Trump knows that — actually, he counts on it. His policies are deeply unpopular, his approval ratings are underwater, and his MAGA base is imploding. Instead of changing what he’s doing, he manufactures constant chaos and distractions, designed to leave people feeling hopeless and keep them from building momentum. But regardless of what the headlines say, the problems we’re facing aren’t going away. The attacks on our rights, working families being squeezed dry at every turn, corporations buying up influence in our elections — they don’t stop because it’s a slow fundraising month. Neither can our work. Here’s the honest truth: getting through this period requires stability. That stability comes from our recurring donors. When we can count on recurring donors to show up every single month, it makes it possible for us to plan ahead, invest early, and fight back consistently. We don’t take money from corporate PACs or special interests. We depend on our grassroots community showing up for us because they truly believe in what we’re building at Leaders We Deserve. And right now, in this slow period, your support means more than ever. Use the links in this email to start a recurring donation to Leaders We Deserve. Thank you, David Hogg

name: Kimberly Bretches
subject: My husband saved Austin. Now he’s facing 99 years in prison.
body:
|
---
[ ](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=b8ca57dc163c4dc4b1a2da91a3d0330c&_e=oqnuvR45135-T1DPCCY3T5cW1XSSJ7LZmUA5Gso5fRnITqsdapPodBq_JwqMqkMFM1tiWR5dvSEVL0JVOTS1fPJqR0c7YhXyAOjGXXa6huXaY1w6ea8S_BJlqEdcLtrbAu9Wn7j60PsQRTyFeO70IxsaxR4T_FmbSVNKR6_dvLzflOauXdbW8gBEAcfJNOkB8WW9N77ogQyFc-6SEr1QWYvsG_rL0hgZpRTQ3Jne47PJ9T0-cFwToOcPSoh3zNAusSC49IIPXWJN9AkPKCiY0UbsUNapMEVGNFgeHkHI6sYfvnYFWnCexxrpRL7_x67lhZxapSE-N7VB7C4BVGVfFwTTGcrI1WsjA8rIkwRHjzYW-MWmiUJLftT0a4-FuIboPCkmM-2qprGETzrwBgHnPDNfYwsKL5o4-bIGlHDtaQdy_zU72GsBmK3vZS5ow4s0) Officer Chance Bretches and his family — Austin, Texas
* * *
Dear Friend, My name is Kimberly Bretches. I'm a wife and a mother of four young children. And I'm writing to you today because I am out of options and I am terrified. I'll be honest, asking strangers for help doesn't come naturally to me. But neither does the idea of my husband spending the rest of his life in prison for doing his job — so here I am. Chance is a police officer in Austin, Texas. He is, without question, the best man I know. He has spent his career keeping dangerous people off the streets, protecting a city that doesn't always make it easy to do so, and coming home every night to a family that needs him. His supervisors have praised him for his professionalism, for his instincts, for his dedication. He is exactly the kind of officer you want showing up when things go wrong. | By every measure, Chance did everything right. He used department-approved tools, following his training, to protect his fellow officers and the people of Austin.
---
And in May of 2020, things went very wrong in Austin. For three consecutive days, our city was under siege. Violent agitators hurled rocks, frozen water bottles, and exploding fireworks directly at police officers. Buildings were vandalized and looted. Police vehicles were set on fire. A rioter was caught with a Molotov cocktail. Officers were injured. The city was teetering on the edge of complete chaos, and everyone knew it.
On May 31st, the third day, when the mob was larger and more dangerous than it had ever been, Chance was on the front lines. He did what he was trained to do. He deployed non-lethal, department-approved beanbag projectiles to stop the aggression and protect his fellow officers and the people of Austin. The crowd eventually dispersed. The city held.
**No one questioned what he did that night. His supervisors praised his response. His department stood behind him.**
And then Austin elected District Attorney José Garza.
Instead of holding the rioters accountable — the ones who threw the fireworks, who lit the fires, who carried the Molotov cocktails — Garza turned his attention to the officers who stopped them. He launched an indiscriminate campaign against law enforcement, using the power of his office not to protect the public, but to make political examples out of good cops.
Chance became one of his targets.
| Chance now faces up to 99 Years in prison — for following his training.
---
My husband now faces up to 99 years in prison. Not because he did anything wrong. Not because his department found fault with his actions. Not because any credible review of that night concluded he acted improperly. But because one politically-motivated prosecutor decided the officers who held the line deserved to be punished for holding it. This case has been grinding through the system for years now. The trial has already been delayed several times and is **now scheduled for June of this year.** Every continuance, every filing, every hearing means more hours of legal work, and more bills our family has to find a way to pay. I am asking for your help because we cannot do this alone. The National Center for Police Defense has been in Chance's corner, and your donation goes toward funding his legal defense, ensuring he has the representation he deserves as he walks into that courtroom in June.
| [DONATE NOW TO SUPPORT CHANCE ›](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=b8ca57dc163c4dc4b1a2da91a3d0330c&_e=oqnuvR45135-T1DPCCY3T5cW1XSSJ7LZmUA5Gso5fRnITqsdapPodBq_JwqMqkMFM1tiWR5dvSEVL0JVOTS1fPJqR0c7YhXyAOjGXXa6huULtXwFO9fzS-4quz3M8U5Mu5_Jpyph4qjRh-ZFQ1UjrDUdbJ9kl9VgV-oAyAn-kVUvkcMuvN5tY18hduEFBLGDGkVmcLvAYmHMgMsPTqxyGZaRHPyfRAGzWIiZeBvrMMUScm69mSRkQKkFA_pdN92sHI3yTukuiupAd71OuETf6TiTzBx7AFwfPfawzFIoQxBS0q6Mbm3WAg3MzEY9ar9xjrSvvoomHXmxp7tW_AaXfYYzLYoCHzEgRc6YafjnnUNDxQ1SkzoPVZ61fvpQ6pP8JMCSOkFx8pGcNBah3a8jyr67kS6nGJiZ7RMtNvr4m59UXbf40uynVFiLr17j1QrZ)
---

If you believe that the men and women who run toward danger deserve better than this, [please give today](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=b8ca57dc163c4dc4b1a2da91a3d0330c&_e=oqnuvR45135-T1DPCCY3T5cW1XSSJ7LZmUA5Gso5fRnITqsdapPodBq_JwqMqkMFM1tiWR5dvSEVL0JVOTS1fPJqR0c7YhXyAOjGXXa6huX-VwjI90phI4LNjfEQPczWcqcS7RAKnB9Uv7blcq5Emzdv9JirAinYKhLvPDCOBb3xdJMeNExlwO1DoeReBhyTtDneiMyi0Vd_eav621gO_rv8hRaArmWGQ3dDhuYnJMewTg2C_3HiqvSS1d0jzHL15FA5u2EKmWnKXOiqmlPCsHhyyW-RAskZwT0Tw1NQLHp6r6VfRXx-mhIR9iiHHxXMrOz9YPVS7wyiTiqyM9SBVU0RGqdlAyHexVxVu8bWCZ5PxYXmdoIlb759IgyzZzqHU_KnVJo_4oU3dQegpE95eajO1aCLcvVcZsND1mFMsGv-llwJwk8xBHUfkGACkmqY). Whatever you can. No amount is too small, and our family will not forget it. Chance stood between Austin and chaos on May 31st, 2020. He didn't hesitate. He didn't run. He did his job. Now I'm asking you to stand with him. With gratitude and hope, **Kimberly Bretches** Wife of Officer Chance Bretches, Austin Police Department
| **P.S.** Chance's trial is this June. We are closer than we've ever been to getting Chance in front of a jury that will hear the truth, but we need the resources to get there. **[Please donate today and help us finish this fight.](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=b8ca57dc163c4dc4b1a2da91a3d0330c&_e=oqnuvR45135-T1DPCCY3T5cW1XSSJ7LZmUA5Gso5fRnITqsdapPodBq_JwqMqkMFM1tiWR5dvSEVL0JVOTS1fPJqR0c7YhXyAOjGXXa6huVQgrzXMz6Qw0EzdstoOrEWiAuNsyweLOCAbbjZGTFsgneVW_9pzO3yVL6p0M4pLbfqLUW5QYg6MwyzPo8ymAA76ERwE0qwSSBKPy6GJAWwo-RAXr-Qy1iOepkSE3SFYnICvKyK9oTXr5n2SFEhmeNFRbxS-vLRqL1BLnSXICJFvICWNxNL520xvQN1G92ooTlQ7NRfEHsel9ifNmdmoDQBMaGeSyGbZgL3Py3tSKgPJYrdtbA-pVux9l2RjAH0D1t-Z-QgJDsBauSwThHJYWSgk7uCCvVsFUkytyrFB1iUXL31kuVjxZsA3bAxaHYxFVD0XJ7dLlglhmlrHgaZzP8e) **

clean_body: | Officer Chance Bretches and his family — Austin, Texas Dear Friend, My name is Kimberly Bretches. I'm a wife and a mother of four young children. And I'm writing to you today because I am out of options and I am terrified. I'll be honest, asking strangers for help doesn't come naturally to me. But neither does the idea of my husband spending the rest of his life in prison for doing his job — so here I am. Chance is a police officer in Austin, Texas. He is, without question, the best man I know. He has spent his career keeping dangerous people off the streets, protecting a city that doesn't always make it easy to do so, and coming home every night to a family that needs him. His supervisors have praised him for his professionalism, for his instincts, for his dedication. He is exactly the kind of officer you want showing up when things go wrong. | By every measure, Chance did everything right. He used department-approved tools, following his training, to protect his fellow officers and the people of Austin. And in May of 2020, things went very wrong in Austin. For three consecutive days, our city was under siege. Violent agitators hurled rocks, frozen water bottles, and exploding fireworks directly at police officers. Buildings were vandalized and looted. Police vehicles were set on fire. A rioter was caught with a Molotov cocktail. Officers were injured. The city was teetering on the edge of complete chaos, and everyone knew it. On May 31st, the third day, when the mob was larger and more dangerous than it had ever been, Chance was on the front lines. He did what he was trained to do. He deployed non-lethal, department-approved beanbag projectiles to stop the aggression and protect his fellow officers and the people of Austin. The crowd eventually dispersed. The city held. No one questioned what he did that night. His supervisors praised his response. His department stood behind him. And then Austin elected District Attorney José Garza. Instead of holding the rioters accountable — the ones who threw the fireworks, who lit the fires, who carried the Molotov cocktails — Garza turned his attention to the officers who stopped them. He launched an indiscriminate campaign against law enforcement, using the power of his office not to protect the public, but to make political examples out of good cops. Chance became one of his targets. | Chance now faces up to 99 Years in prison — for following his training. My husband now faces up to 99 years in prison. Not because he did anything wrong. Not because his department found fault with his actions. Not because any credible review of that night concluded he acted improperly. But because one politically-motivated prosecutor decided the officers who held the line deserved to be punished for holding it. This case has been grinding through the system for years now. The trial has already been delayed several times and is now scheduled for June of this year. Every continuance, every filing, every hearing means more hours of legal work, and more bills our family has to find a way to pay. I am asking for your help because we cannot do this alone. The National Center for Police Defense has been in Chance's corner, and your donation goes toward funding his legal defense, ensuring he has the representation he deserves as he walks into that courtroom in June. | · · · or any amount you can give If you believe that the men and women who run toward danger deserve better than this, . Whatever you can. No amount is too small, and our family will not forget it. Chance stood between Austin and chaos on May 31st, 2020. He didn't hesitate. He didn't run. He did his job. Now I'm asking you to stand with him. With gratitude and hope, Kimberly Bretches Wife of Officer Chance Bretches, Austin Police Department | P.S. Chance's trial is this June. We are closer than we've ever been to getting Chance in front of a jury that will hear the truth, but we need the resources to get there. ** National Center for Police Defense · Defenders for Justice

name: The DNC
subject: This is the most strategic donation you can make
body:
---
Derek, if you’ve been looking for the most strategic donation you can make during the 2026 midterm elections, this is it. Here’s why your $7 goes furthest when you [**donate to the DNC:**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-may-2026-gfr?amount=7&refcode=em_20260517_7005_fr&refcode2=7005_22930333_cmaWuA&akid=7005%2E22930333%2EcmaWuA) We are the _only_ committee focused on electing Democrats at every single level of government. That includes candidates for your local school board, Democrats running in Pennsylvania, and beyond. It’s our job to send money to candidates who would benefit from that funding the most. And there’s more: Across the country, we’re working hard to reach Democrats who don't vote in every election — our voters — people who will help us win if they show up at the polls in November. Think about the friends or relatives you have who are Democrats but who don't always vote. There are millions of people like them across the country, and traditionally, getting them to show up in a midterm year is even tougher. If we can persuade them to vote in November’s elections, we can win the midterms. [**That’s exactly how we’ll put that $7 contribution to work: Reaching voters. Investing in campaigns where more funding can make a huge difference. Putting Democrats in the best possible position to take back Congress this fall, and ultimately, the White House. If this is important to you, we’re asking you to make that contribution now:**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-may-2026-gfr?amount=7&refcode=em_20260517_7005_fr&refcode2=7005_22930333_cmaWuA&akid=7005%2E22930333%2EcmaWuA) _If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly:_ | | | **[CONTRIBUTE $7](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-may-2026-gfr?express_lane=true&amount=7&refcode=em_20260517_7005_fr&refcode2=7005_22930333_cmaWuA&akid=7005%2E22930333%2EcmaWuA)**

Thank you in advance for your help,
The Democrats

_If you gave recently via check or mail, our system may not have yet processed your gift. We are appreciative and thank you greatly for your support._

| | | The Trump administration is now preparing to dismantle one of the world's most important science laboratories.
---
This lab helps scientists predict hurricanes, wildfires, floods, and extreme weather events that threaten communities across our country. But instead of listening to scientists, this administration is attacking them.

This is part of a pattern we’ve seen again and again: undermine research, silence experts, and pretend facts don’t matter. **I’m Dr. Jasmine Clark. I have a PhD in microbiology. I’ve spent my career studying science, teaching science, and fighting for evidence-based solutions to the challenges our communities face.**

clean_body: They're dismantling science| | | = | | | The Trump administration is now preparing to dismantle one of the world's most important science laboratories. This lab helps scientists predict hurricanes, wildfires, floods, and extreme weather events that threaten communities across our country. But instead of listening to scientists, this administration is attacking them. This is part of a pattern we’ve seen again and again: undermine research, silence experts, and pretend facts don’t matter. I’m Dr. Jasmine Clark. I have a PhD in microbiology. I’ve spent my career studying science, teaching science, and fighting for evidence-based solutions to the challenges our communities face. = I’m running for Congress because we need more scientists in Washington right now — not politicians who treat science like an inconvenience. = = Science matters. Facts matter. And the future of our communities depends on leaders who understand that. Thank you for standing with me in this fight. Dr. Jasmine Clark = | | | | | | | ---|---|---|--- Paid for by Jasmine Clark for Georgia Jasmine Clark for Georgia 4370 Lawrenceville Hwy #96 Lilburn, GA 30048 United States If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please .

## The Capitalist exists because of you, and we want to hear from you!
**[Become a paid subscriber today and access the Live Capitalist chat](https://substack.com/redirect/b1a8ea2e-043a-4264-b387-2ea4bc8513a1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
[ Subscribe here now](https://substack.com/redirect/b1a8ea2e-043a-4264-b387-2ea4bc8513a1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
* * *
I am not surprised Christopher Nolan cast Lupita Nyong'o as Helen of Troy. He had no choice if he wanted an Oscar.
It does not matter that Homer in his 2800 year-old poem _The Iliad_ described Helen as "white-armed" and "fair-haired". White-armed meaning her wealth and status shielded her from the harshness of the sun. Fair-haired... obvious.
Miss Nyong'o is beautiful as Helen was said to be beautiful, but she is neither "light armed" nor "fair haired". It does not matter. What matters is diversity.
Diversity is all that matters.
We are forgetting some of the diversity mandates thrust upon society and institutions after George Floyd's overdose. (This line always gets me in trouble but read the coroner's report if you think I'm being flippant or crass...)
We saw the frantic scramble to tear down statues nationwide, not just of Confederates and not just in the South, but of Washington and Jefferson, of Teddy Roosevelt at New York's Natural History Museum, of Saint Francis of Assisi in San Francisco's Golden Gate Park (wut...?) of famed abolitionist Colonel Hans Christian Heg at the Wisconsin state house (huh...?).
Mobs aren't always known for their brains.
Friends of mine, names you might know from TV and media, stood guard at the historic statue of Abraham Lincoln in Washington DC's Lincoln Park determined to protect it. They succeeded.
Along with the toppling of statues of bad white people came the banning of images of real black people. Aunt Jemima and Uncle Ben, real people who had their real faces on their real products from which their real descendants made real royalties... gone. Fictional brands like the smiling chef on Cream of Wheat or the grandmotherly Mrs. Butterworths also had to go. Black were now like the prophet Mohammad and depictions of them had us all being at risk of the same fate as the staff of Charlie Hebdo.
Racism was being eliminated one food group at a time.
* * *
* * *
**Sponsored by:[Mode Mobile](https://substack.com/redirect/9b884278-66a9-4b7c-904c-1b3202dbe8fe?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
# [The Next Breakout Might Be in Your Pocket](https://substack.com/redirect/9b884278-66a9-4b7c-904c-1b3202dbe8fe?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/9b884278-66a9-4b7c-904c-1b3202dbe8fe?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Everyone's hunting for the next Unicorn.
The type of "category disruptor" that grows fast and turns early believers into big winners.
59,000+ investors think that**[Mode Mobile](https://substack.com/redirect/9b884278-66a9-4b7c-904c-1b3202dbe8fe?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) **could be one of those rare finds.
Americans spend 4 1⁄2 hours on their phones daily, and Mode Mobile is monetizing that screentime. With **[$1B+ earned by over 490M customers](https://substack.com/redirect/9b884278-66a9-4b7c-904c-1b3202dbe8fe?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** and 32,481% revenue growth, Mode's EarnPhone is turning smartphones into income generating assets.
Their previous raises sold out, and the company is now offering pre-IPO shares at $0.50/share with up to 20% bonus, exclusive to early investors.
Being early is everything, and this window is still open.
**[Review the offer before it closes](https://substack.com/redirect/9b884278-66a9-4b7c-904c-1b3202dbe8fe?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
[ Click here](https://substack.com/redirect/9b884278-66a9-4b7c-904c-1b3202dbe8fe?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
* * *
Eliminating was only half the battle. Proactive steps were needed: Joe Biden's Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) mandated racial diversity requirement for corporations to be eligible for listing on the Stock Exchange. The National College Athletic Association (NCAA) required racial diversity in hiring practices for league accreditation. And the Academy of Motion Pictures Arts and Sciences which awards the prestigious Oscars required that every nominee for best picture must feature an actor of color in a lead role.
Retroactively, this means epic best picture winners like the _Godfather, Schindler 's List, Ordinary People, The Wizard of Oz_, or _Titanic_ would no longer be eligible for an Oscar nomination. Going forward it means Helen of Troy is now Helen of Timbuktu.
But that does not matter. What matters is diversity whether it is anachronistic insanity or just poor taste like one of those TV cooking shows where the chefs must make a dessert and the ingredients are anchovies, roquefort, and Tang. Yes, chef, you made a trifle that no one wants to eat and tastes disgusting. First prize! Like the novice designer who throws pieces together, a lava lamp on a Second Empire credenza with the painting of dogs playing poker, and calling it "eclectic". It's crap.
Diversity is good because it is good. A tautology of liberalism, a self-fulfilling prophecy of racial justice fulfilling racial justice with racial justice.
For famed movie director Christopher Nolan, who has made some of my favorite films in the last 20 years (_Memento, Dunkirk, Inception_) his roughly $200 million project is not going to risk an Oscar nomination because he did not play Hollywood's game. You want a racial quota, you got it. So poor Miss Nyong'o... a quota. How very cruel.
The left is very cruel.
Pro football coach Anthony Lynn famously refuses to interview for an open role if he knows he is not seriously being considered. The NFL's "Rooney Rule" requires teams to interview a certain number of minority candidates, and Lynn, who is black, has said publicly he is not going to be a "token". This is man who thinks too highly of himself and his talent to be made a fool... what a man. A model man. I bet he would have stood guard at the Lincoln statue.
I don't blame Ms. Nyong'o for taking the role. Heck, I'd probably take the role if offered it. And why can't Helen of Troy be a 50-year-old 6'3 bald male farmer? Though Helen is not going to happen, one role I did want to play in my younger days when I thought I had an acting bug was Stage Manager in Thornton Wilder's beloved American play _Our Town_. We had just read it in High School, and the theater program was considering a production. I still remember rehearsing a scene for the audition some 36 years later.
On a work trip to New York some years ago, I saw the marquis for a new adaptation on Broadway and considered buying a ticket. The lanky nerd from the TV show _The Big Bang Theory_ was cast in my dream role. I was curious to hear the lines... but fortunately I read a review.
Woke Broadway had found Thornton Wilder lacking
The existential sorrow of the plot was not enough. Broadway needed to introduce race as well. I refused to go. Mind you, I have no problem with theater about racial issues. In that same high school English Department, we read also _Fences, A Raisin in the Sun, The Color Purple_ , and with a "diverse" class of New York City students, discussed the issues together.
Some dingdong adding race to this masterpiece is like inserting a banjo into Mozart's _Requiem_. It simply does not belong. And how dare anyone think he can do this to someone else's masterpiece. What lack of humility and respect.
The left is not humble and has no respect.
A play I did see on another trip to NYC was the S. E. Hinton autobiography _The Outsiders_ about rival teen gangs in mid-1960's Oklahoma. We had also read that. (Boy, I had some excellent English teachers...) although we also had the help of Francis Ford Coppola movie with perhaps the most impressive cast of young actors destined for stardom ever assembled. Broadway had turned _The Outsiders_ it into a musical (hmmm...). I did not read a review, bought a last-minute ticket, and surprise!
Woke Broadway had found S.E. Hinton, also, lacking.
Race. Everything race. As Marx reduced everything to class struggle, modern Marxists see the world through the race struggle, and where race does not exist, it must be inserted. The boys of _The Outsiders_ , Dallas, Pony Boy, Johnny, Soda Pop... were real people and Hinton told their story, but they lacked something crucial, the most important part of anyone's identity: skin color. And their rivalry was no longer cultural or socio-economic, rich kids "Socs" vs. poor kids "Greasers". It was now race, because race is diverse, and diversity is good because it is.
"An appeaser is one who feeds a crocodile, hoping it will eat him last," said the great Winston Churchill. Our statutes, our art, our culture, our history, thrown to monsters, hoping to survive the attack. "Maybe they will leave me standing. Maybe they won't come for me. Maybe if I throw them enough chum, they will not eat me." So very cowardly. So very sad.
You are not wrong for accepting Helen of Troy as a black woman. You are not wrong for being annoyed or even just blatantly tired of it. The question is how far we are pushed until there is nothing left to preserve.
I think of those friends of mine, names you might know from TV and media, who stood guard at the historic statue of Abraham Lincoln in Washington DC's Lincoln Park determined to protect it. They succeeded.
The mob will always back down if you stand willing to fight. Let us do so while some of the statutes and our history and culture still remain.
**Honor and courage.**
* * *
_**Daniel Turner is the founder and executive director of Power The Future, a national nonprofit organization that advocates for American energy jobs. He also runs a sheep and cattle farm in rural Virginia. Contact him at daniel@powerthefuture.com and follow him on Twitter @DanielTurnerPTF**_

name: Melat Kiros
subject: The Colorado governor just commuted an election denier's sentence, I'm calling for impeachment hearings.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kiros for Congress](https://cefuo8hbb
body:
| | | | | [](https://cefuo8hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001u4zikq9JNKgZ55wcmoDoVQVlEXU1iK-JzWJiPX_IkuuLBfpxM9X1Q1lcc7zHHXN6_cluPobpg8tT3_Y5A_JAqpTsZTIWraC3GgzcYITM22i7IM5EsE41zQ_rMR0zhnsRgxOqwA41p9dQL4WDAqdDe7NYe-lopJGejcHaOYzmEljXTZ0jSLXyt03K8gBMe2_qyueLcJONJMoXO9VM-btlyKDAfg3SRLO0N4ZlS-5l7vhTqthoqO4ogVl82jPAudJdlWQJBWJF5jnrBooo_4a19UoMZzOKo1pNOTJeXSVMSfo=&c=sFFEKIRU7y52zsAqKWu0kwxLJwqPVrxbnzthpjxBPibV6fNOnuRfBw==&ch=TCwvyes7yN8H3T2PdoNe2_X0z7MhrO4Ra-NwseyGL0LstRj4RllTwQ==)
---
| This isn't the email I wanted to write to you this Sunday, but I'm not running for Congress to be another politician who turns a blind eye to corruption.

So, with that in mind, I want to address the recent actions of our Governor:
Tina Peters was convicted on seven counts, including four felonies, for allowing unauthorized access to Mesa County's voting system, lying to investigators, and obstructing the Colorado Secretary of State. She was sentenced to nine years by a jury of her peers. The Republican prosecutor who put her there called it the most aggravated attempt to influence a public servant he had ever seen.
While Governor Polis has the authority to commute that sentence, he expressed no interest in doing so until Donald Trump pressured him to. Like so many of our elected leaders in this moment, the governor chose appeasement and capitulation over democracy.
A governor who uses a constitutional power to assist an unlawful federal coercion scheme has violated his oath to defend the U.S. Constitution and Colorado's independence. This is grounds for impeachment.
I am calling for an immediate investigation into any collusion between Jared Polis and the Trump administration. I am also calling on the Colorado State Legislature to convene a special session to consider articles of impeachment against Governor Polis.
The election officials of Colorado fought honorably to protect your vote. Jared Polis undid that work by buckling to Trump's threat to withhold public funds from hungry Colorado children, clean water, and disaster relief.
My generation has seen the consequences of Democrats not standing up for justice, and we refuse to continue down this path. We are sick of corporate politicians putting their donors, ambition, and privilege ahead of real people. The answer to authoritarianism is not accommodation. This corruption cannot go unanswered because the only path out of this authoritarianism is by holding those accountable at every step of the way.
Donald Trump's corrupt acts compound one after the other, and if we allow this corruption to go unanswered, it will erode what little is left of our democracy.
We need leaders who will stand firm and be unafraid to call out such capitulation, and that's who I intend to be as your next Congresswoman.
- Melat Kiros
---
|

clean_body: | | | | | == | This isn't the email I wanted to write to you this Sunday, but I'm not running for Congress to be another politician who turns a blind eye to corruption. So, with that in mind, I want to address the recent actions of our Governor: Tina Peters was convicted on seven counts, including four felonies, for allowing unauthorized access to Mesa County's voting system, lying to investigators, and obstructing the Colorado Secretary of State. She was sentenced to nine years by a jury of her peers. The Republican prosecutor who put her there called it the most aggravated attempt to influence a public servant he had ever seen. While Governor Polis has the authority to commute that sentence, he expressed no interest in doing so until Donald Trump pressured him to. Like so many of our elected leaders in this moment, the governor chose appeasement and capitulation over democracy. A governor who uses a constitutional power to assist an unlawful federal coercion scheme has violated his oath to defend the U.S. Constitution and Colorado's independence. This is grounds for impeachment. I am calling for an immediate investigation into any collusion between Jared Polis and the Trump administration. I am also calling on the Colorado State Legislature to convene a special session to consider articles of impeachment against Governor Polis. The election officials of Colorado fought honorably to protect your vote. Jared Polis undid that work by buckling to Trump's threat to withhold public funds from hungry Colorado children, clean water, and disaster relief. My generation has seen the consequences of Democrats not standing up for justice, and we refuse to continue down this path. We are sick of corporate politicians putting their donors, ambition, and privilege ahead of real people. The answer to authoritarianism is not accommodation. This corruption cannot go unanswered because the only path out of this authoritarianism is by holding those accountable at every step of the way. Donald Trump's corrupt acts compound one after the other, and if we allow this corruption to go unanswered, it will erode what little is left of our democracy. We need leaders who will stand firm and be unafraid to call out such capitulation, and that's who I intend to be as your next Congresswoman. - Melat Kiros | | | == | Melat Kiros is a Democrat, daughter of Ethiopian immigrants, and community advocate running for Congress in Colorado's 1st District. She's challenging a 30-year incumbent because she believes Denver deserves fresh, energetic leadership that understands today's challenges -- from the cost of living to money in politics -- and isn't afraid to fight for bold solutions. | | == | | == | | | | Melat Kiros | PO Box 16134 | Denver, CO 80216 US | |

name: Voter Action Project Research Team
subject: Go back to single day voting?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
You are invited to join our one-click issue response poll.
For data integrity purposes, please do not complete this survey if your email address is not dpwillis67@gmail.com .
Team: Cleta Mitchell -- a Trump lawyer, anti-voter conspiracy theorist, and vote suppressor -- and other activists from a top anti-voting group with close ties to the White House are pushing plans to severely limit early and mail voting .
Those potential cutbacks, which echo what President Donald Trump has called for in speeches and executive orders, would radically curtail access to the ballot, taking the U.S. back decades to the era of one-day elections . Cleta Mitchell reported to fellow activists afterwards that the group wants nearly all voting to take place in person, on Election Day.
Their plan would dramatically limit in-person early voting, which has helped make it easier for voters who work long hours or lack flexibility in their schedule to cast a ballot. Mail-in voting also would be curtailed under their plan, and the plan would even bar local election officials from printing additional ballots if they run out on Election Day.
We're looking to see how our supporters feel about this effort. Please take our quick survey:
Team: Do you agree with Trump and Mitchell that we should go back to the era of single day elections?
By clicking a response below your answer will be automatically recorded:
YES [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-pollresponse?refcode=051726_1yes&amount=15] NO [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-pollresponse?refcode=051726_1no&amount=15]
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
https://www.facebook.com/voteraction [https://www.facebook.com/voteraction] https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org [https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org] https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/ [https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/] https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/ [https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/]
Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=051726_1&amount=20]
Donate Now [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=051726_1&amount=20]

name: Wes Moore HQ
subject: “Things are made to happen”
body:
# [](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/8?t=1&akid=1398%2E1086043%2EzHh4mC)
Peter, we have to use every single minute of every single day to fight for results for the people of Maryland.
**Wes Moore knows it:** It’s why he keeps a countdown clock on his desk that counts down the days left in his term. Like he says, “Things don’t happen. _Things are made to happen.”_
**It’s how he’s been able to get so much done:** cutting utility costs for Maryland families, making our communities safer, record investments in public schools, and bringing jobs and new housing to our state.
[**But with less than 6 months until Election Day, we have to stay focused on the fight ahead to continue that progress. Will you chip in any amount to power our re-election effort today?**](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/417?c0=wes-em-20260517-1.xigFMR&amount=5&refcode=em-20260517&t=2&akid=1398%2E1086043%2EzHh4mC)
**[Donate $5 ››](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/417?c0=wes-em-20260517-1.xigFMR&amount=5&refcode=em-20260517&t=21&akid=1398%2E1086043%2EzHh4mC)**
Let’s make sure we don’t reset the clock on our accomplishments.
Together,
Wes Moore HQ

* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-footer?refcode=em-20260517&t=22&refcode2=1398_1086043_zHh4mC&akid=1398%2E1086043%2EzHh4mC)**
**[Volunteer »](https://support.wesmoore.com/a/volunteer?source=em-20260517)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.wesmoore.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=23&akid=1398%2E1086043%2EzHh4mC)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.wesmoore.com/signup/update-your-information/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.wesmoore.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=24&akid=1398%2E1086043%2EzHh4mC)

Email us: [info@wesmoore.com](mailto:info@wesmoore.com)
BY AUTHORITY: WES MOORE FOR MARYLAND, MARY TYDINGS, TREASURER

Let’s make sure we don’t reset the clock on our accomplishments. Together, Wes Moore HQ

name: Aaron Ford
subject: A ton of money
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Committee to Elect Aaron Ford
body:
# [](https://act.fordfornevada.com/go/12?t=1&akid=134%2E127862%2EO20hEY)
Republicans have built a war chest of _$14 million_ to protect MAGA Gov. Joe Lombardo.
In the first three months of this year alone, Lombardo raised $3.7 million — including a $1 million check from one of Donald Trump's biggest backers.
They’ll likely outspend us. I won't pretend otherwise.
But we have something they can’t buy: Energy.
**Our campaign already broke the record for grassroots donations by a non-incumbent Nevada gubernatorial candidate.**
One donor can write Lombardo a million-dollar check, but they can't manufacture the people-powered movement we're building, and the polls show it:
[](https://act.fordfornevada.com/go/1?c0=em_ms_af_20260517_1_ak.D64WRn&amount=5&refcode=em20260517-112&t=2&akid=134%2E127862%2EO20hEY)
**Despite Lombardo’s cash advantage, this race is statistically tied. That means we're in this, and we can win it.**
[**We just need to keep showing up. Can you chip in $5 today to help us keep connecting with Nevada families across the state?**](https://act.fordfornevada.com/go/1?c0=em_ms_af_20260517_1_ak.D64WRn&amount=5&refcode=em20260517-112&t=3&akid=134%2E127862%2EO20hEY)
**[Donate $5 ››](https://act.fordfornevada.com/go/1?c0=em_ms_af_20260517_1_ak.D64WRn&amount=5&refcode=em20260517-112&t=22&akid=134%2E127862%2EO20hEY)**
Thank you,
Aaron

_What if_ I told you that a recent poll shows me 1 point down in this race?

Last night, we fell short of our fundraising goals for our final legally required fundraising deadline before the primary.
It’s not great to say the least.
With our final budget decisions before the primary on the horizon, and our opponents pummeling me with baseless attacks, we cannot afford this shortfall.
So we urgently need to catch up today. It’s the only way we can continue reaching undecided voters and make sure our Republican opponents don’t lock Democrats out of the general election on June 2nd.
[**Can you please rush whatever you can afford to my campaign today? name: Tyler Vandenberg
subject: The MAGA Extremist in “Independent’s” Clothing
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY RESIST RECLAIM REBUILD PAC (WWW
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rrr-vandenberg [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rrr-vandenberg]
In March, Kevin Kiley held a press conference and announced he was leaving the Republican Party. He called himself an Independent. He wants you to believe he's a changed man — a brave moderate willing to break from the MAGA crowd.
Don't buy it. Not for a single second.
Kiley didn't leave MAGA. MAGA left him without a safe district after California redrew its maps, and now he's trying to trick his way into a new one. That's not a political evolution. That's a survival strategy — and a cynical one at that.
His record tells the whole story.
When Trump needed votes for the Big Beautiful Bill — 887 pages of Medicaid cuts, gutted SNAP benefits, shredded clean energy programs, and $3.3 trillion added to the national debt — Kevin Kiley was there.
He delivered for Trump when it counted most, and then had the audacity to put out a press release calling it "historic tax relief for Californians."
Time and again, when Donald Trump and Mike Johnson have needed a vote, they've known they could count on Kevin Kiley. The independent label is something he put on in March 2026 — right after the maps changed and his old seat became impossible to hold. He didn't evolve. He rebranded.
I'm a Marine Corps veteran, a Harvard Kennedy School graduate, and someone who has spent his entire career in service to this country. When I get to Congress, I won't be looking for a convenient label to hide behind. I'll be fighting to restore what Kiley helped tear down: robust Medicaid protections, strong public land preservation, and a government that actually works for the people who live here — not the MAGA donors who fund campaigns like his.
Kevin Kiley had his chance. He chose Trump's agenda over California's families. Every single time.
Now it's our turn to choose. Chip in today and help us take this seat. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rrr-vandenberg]
DONATE TODAY TO HELP WIN THIS RACE! [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rrr-vandenberg]
Let's get to work.
— Tyler Vandenberg
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rrr-vandenberg [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rrr-vandenberg]

body:

Peter,
---
It's Gary Sinise here. You may know me from my roles in movies like Apollo 13 _ _ and The Green Mile or the CBS television series CSI: NY - but it was in 1994 playing the role of Lt. Dan in Forrest Gump that changed my life and inspired my enduring connection to our defenders, veterans, and first responders. I've served as an Honorary Grand Marshal for the National Memorial Day Parade in Washington, D.C. nearly every year since American Veterans Center revived it in 2005. **But I've never been more excited to serve as an Honorary Grand Marshall than I am this year - the year of America's 250****th**** birthday.**
---
[](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=b9150752597248cbab6ae5f50b9ef921&_e=XNujkrU-t_oWoFoz6DBVpr_EIrUliOAHiPDuTFmzGemw0OJ4Vzl3bVtz-ArMmxzfX8SdNUdIMXpZW86w-MzRkRGDTc2YXG9-Kj8Lr6r7hQzsb6E9v0zQ_-PvDPagzfMgsVlPd76ZCmdLeJuazCpUXSz9QoDk5ShJgBo389lL_S-Cx1k19eSYoUNqS0dvZd8LqyRiNUZZvMTZHR3kck7-xtQqoPZ8Rshuc7E_K7VhvckfYi4qql3qOwyANG_-BaLL8VFN5t2WTKZYTVI8aZK52KRuw3Z-5UY_oAx9GuGWWmO3FhrqJM2NlwK7q-nDqhAakmo3mtDICNXuBnfAMCo6zEl0ectwHvO9sm4bB3dXRB3XncW8O892plMhZoGBNw3QVyDgiWSk7xUbeL_0LK7OFGUCZoaY-w9VfsXHtvm95LQcxixP0cSrNCREwtzdN1pQFIfCnXDckSkD7eOsBCKREVI5fv0fUM0fMTu98qeN1Gfe6sgyUG_VKFbvpRwuzTTj0rLJoncG2fDde8E1xBbNUN2MjJ0IDwpe2cpLFrD-pGEbvARL4PoTALEacBEooRwbnO84n_FCQXFne7e6oE-Fxi0Vj_1A7RG3gvCGcpG34bWMJN8mUVCz52imaHQi6a3qvhE854LDoIancQXJt6lVx_FI0SuvQNJ5uUS-n4s8DKbrDLXsN_ohg0rswrxKRRRRN2ownTxleDR3PtG_dwpx0g%3D%3D)

Peter, this Memorial Day marks the first major national act of remembrance of the veterans who made the ultimate sacrifice to defend America from its enemies and ensure it has remained the Land of the Free for the last 250 years. And this year's National Memorial Day Parade will be a special edition - the biggest parade yet - where national memory meets living history as veterans from every military generation celebrate America and honor the service and sacrifice of their fallen brethren. **Peter, the National Memorial Day Parade is ENIRELY FUNDED by private contributions, including from generous patriots like you.** **[In light of America’s 250th birthday and the special opportunity we have to honor veterans this year, will you chip in today to help support the parade with a gift of $25, $50, $75, $150, or more?](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=b9150752597248cbab6ae5f50b9ef921&_e=XNujkrU-t_oWoFoz6DBVpr_EIrUliOAHiPDuTFmzGemw0OJ4Vzl3bVtz-ArMmxzfX8SdNUdIMXpZW86w-MzRkRGDTc2YXG9-Kj8Lr6r7hQzsb6E9v0zQ_-PvDPagzfMgsVlPd76ZCmdLeJuazCpUXSz9QoDk5ShJgBo389lL_S-Cx1k19eSYoUNqS0dvZd8LqyRiNUZZvMTZHR3kck7-xtQqoPZ8Rshuc7E_K7VhvckfYi4qql3qOwyANG_-BaLLWAmwY1JZ9qR31be_jh0P7yh3PfFAjMhggAdy9rLFcCPi8teQU_DkCOzNXciGCaKpYq_MPbnLT897jmNMdH1bMMSy5sp1_6-3fETBrUxzU7AmsahCOIy4i_NyQYktPv7U3fVgbZy95bt64V5bqXyAT8-qLH6adDawo4TMRs4lVwssq-MZ2EWUiGgucQ8E2aKamJSJR4p33NxNetPuI5wat-gH_l-_2uHX5u0I1iorjVQTt5GxZMer7oCeO4-mTL07XbTYR9WixvJMj1hqcpbwiBKnkdsCOUAPA-rR7pnxrJq7a15-dC65SfGoE58WlHQLp4dpeW8e1PZQHaSMlsKsh8_P_ab7ztvQGJGFPKFPubVv2jyhh8Naiq1-DfD72EgG7YLlHYqq9pkqDTW3XRLB7euk02NgOY9BzCKQo4Z9-C-_GcL0uz-IXGWiRmzhwebZvvO7odlcnqaMqgJJ-CzZEg%3D%3D)**

Come to the National Memorial Day Parade in Washington, D.C. Visit a local veterans' cemetery. Send a note to a family of the fallen. Or simply take a quiet moment to think about the men and women who gave their lives to defend OUR country. clean_body: The biggest National Memorial Day Parade yet It's Gary Sinise here. | | | | Peter, It's Gary Sinise here. You may know me from my roles in movies like Apollo 13 and The Green Mile or the CBS television series CSI: NY - but it was in 1994 playing the role of Lt. Dan in Forrest Gump that changed my life and inspired my enduring connection to our defenders, veterans, and first responders. I've served as an Honorary Grand Marshal for the National Memorial Day Parade in Washington, D.C. nearly every year since American Veterans Center revived it in 2005. But I've never been more excited to serve as an Honorary Grand Marshall than I am this year - the year of America's 250th birthday. Peter, this Memorial Day marks the first major national act of remembrance of the veterans who made the ultimate sacrifice to defend America from its enemies and ensure it has remained the Land of the Free for the last 250 years. And this year's National Memorial Day Parade will be a special edition - the biggest parade yet - where national memory meets living history as veterans from every military generation celebrate America and honor the service and sacrifice of their fallen brethren. Peter, the National Memorial Day Parade is ENIRELY FUNDED by private contributions, including from generous patriots like you. WWII veterans were a key part of the first parade when it was revived in 2005, as they have been every year since for the last 20 years. But this may be the last year we have the opportunity to have these brave men and women with us for the parade. | | | | | | | | This year is a rare moment when living witnesses to World War II and America 's 250th birthday overlap. And no matter how much you can give to this important cause, I want to ask something additional of you. On Memorial Day - not just this momentous year of military milestones, but every year - please join me in honoring those who made the ultimate sacrifice for our freedoms. Come to the National Memorial Day Parade in Washington, D.C. Visit a local veterans' cemetery. Send a note to a family of the fallen. Or simply take a quiet moment to think about the men and women who gave their lives to defend OUR country. I believe that while we can never do enough to express our gratitude to our defenders, veterans, and first responders, we can always do a little more. Peter, thank you for your support of America 's heroes. Sincerely, Tim Holbert President American Veterans Center | | | The mission of the American Veterans Center and the World War II Veterans Committee is to preserve and promote the legacy and experiences of America's veterans and active duty service personnel from World War II through today. A non-profit educational organization, the AVC and WWIIVC are funded solely through generous contributions from people like you. All contributions are tax-deductible. (C) American Veterans Center 1100 N. Glebe Road Suite 910, Arlington, VA 22201

name: Team Pramila
subject: Sign the petition: Tell Trump to end his inhumane Cuban fuel blockade
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Pramila for Congress
body:
Pramila isn’t backing down. And neither are we.
# [](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/892?refcode=em20260517-6244-footer&t=1&akid=6244%2E420207%2EUPw_N9)
Peter,
One of Trump’s far-right operatives, Laura Loomer, recently called for Pramila’s arrest on social media — simply for doing her job in Congress.
[](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/sign/em20260517_cuba_petition/?source=em20260517-6244&t=2&akid=6244%2E420207%2EUPw_N9)
Here’s what actually happened:
As a Member of Congress, Pramila met with ambassadors from other countries to discuss the real-world impacts of U.S. foreign policy. That’s it.
**But Trump’s MAGA network is now spreading lies and escalating attacks because Pramila spoke out about the humanitarian crisis caused by Trump’s fuel blockade of Cuba.**
This is what they do — they target people who challenge them, spread misinformation to drown out the truth, and try to intimidate leaders into silence.
But Pramila isn’t backing down. And neither are we.
[**If you’re with us, will you add your name right now to stand up for the Cuban people and end Trump’s cruel, inhumane blockade?**](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/sign/em20260517_cuba_petition/?source=em20260517-6244&t=3&akid=6244%2E420207%2EUPw_N9)
**[Sign The Petition](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/sign/em20260517_cuba_petition/?source=em20260517-6244&t=4&akid=6244%2E420207%2EUPw_N9)**
Thanks for standing with us,
Team Pramila
**[Contribute](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-footer?refcode=em20260517-6244-footer&t=5&refcode2=6244_420207_UPw_N9&akid=6244%2E420207%2EUPw_N9)**
---
[](https://www.facebook.com/pramila.jayapal) | [](https://twitter.com/pramilajayapal) | [](https://www.instagram.com/pramilajayapal/)
Email us: info@pramilaforcongress.com
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, and small-dollar contributions sustain our movement for transformative change. To contribute by check, please address to Pramila for Congress, PO Box 21912, Seattle, WA 98111.

* [Update My Personal Information](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/signup/did-your-info-change/?t=6&akid=6244%2E420207%2EUPw_N9)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=7&akid=6244%2E420207%2EUPw_N9)

This decision was a turning point in our nation’s ongoing fight for civil rights and equality. Today is a reminder that the progress we have fought so hard for must continue to be defended and protected by every generation. At a time in our country when we are hearing dangerous rhetoric and racist scapegoating from the Trump administration, seeing attacks on diversity and inclusion initiatives, and watching hard-fought civil rights protections come under attack, the legacy of _Brown v. Board of Education_ remains as important as ever. I will always remain committed to protecting equal opportunity, defending civil rights, and ensuring every child has access to a quality education. But this work does not happen on its own. As extremists continue working to undermine decades of progress, we must continue building the resources necessary to fight back and protect our shared values. **[If you are able, please consider making a contribution today to support my work in Congress and help us continue defending civil rights, public education, and opportunity for clean_body: Seventy-two years ago, the Supreme Court issued a landmark ruling that racial segregation in public schools was unconstitutional. | | | Friend, Seventy-two years ago, the Supreme Court issued a landmark ruling that racial segregation in public schools was unconstitutional. This decision was a turning point in our nation’s ongoing fight for civil rights and equality. Today is a reminder that the progress we have fought so hard for must continue to be defended and protected by every generation. At a time in our country when we are hearing dangerous rhetoric and racist scapegoating from the Trump administration, seeing attacks on diversity and inclusion initiatives, and watching hard-fought civil rights protections come under attack, the legacy of Brown v. Board of Education remains as important as ever. I will always remain committed to protecting equal opportunity, defending civil rights, and ensuring every child has access to a quality education. But this work does not happen on its own. As extremists continue working to undermine decades of progress, we must continue building the resources necessary to fight back and protect our shared values. Hello – it’s Eileen Laubacher, the Democrat and retired U.S. Navy Admiral running against Lauren Boebert – [**and in a moment, I am going to ask you to make a monthly donation to my campaign to help me defeat Lauren Boebert and flip this seat blue. But before I do, please let me explain why your donation today is so important.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/131582365/621603443/156431970?refcode=20260517-nd&recurring=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjIvMS8xMTgxNzIiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYTRjZDEyYWYtMjI1Mi1mMTExLThlZjItMDAwZDNhMTRiNjQwIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=sobzwYLPEX2lZ_wdi9J0yR_rGoF2ojqz0jjVVQNVI00=)
I never thought I’d run for Congress. After 34 years in the Navy, I retired planning to spend a lot more time with my husband and our kids. But when I see our constitution and our democracy under attack the way they are right now by people like Lauren Boebert, there was no answer other than to try to serve once again.
We can defeat Lauren Boebert and finally end her embarrassing political career. Adam Frisch only lost to Boebert by 546 votes in what was the closest race in the entire country. She is not invincible.
And if we do defeat Boebert and flip this seat blue, Democrats are basically guaranteed to take back the House this November!
But Lauren Boebert has taken millions of dollars from corporate PACs and the likes of Elon Musk. **If we are going to be able to fight back against the Super PAC lies and win, I am counting on grassroots donors like you to chip in to my campaign now.**
[**Please, will you make a monthly donation to my campaign right now – no amount is too small – to help me defeat Lauren Boebert, flip this seat blue, and take back the House for Democrats?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/131582366/621603445/156431970?refcode=20260517-nd&recurring=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjIvMS8xMTgxNzIiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYTRjZDEyYWYtMjI1Mi1mMTExLThlZjItMDAwZDNhMTRiNjQwIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=sobzwYLPEX2lZ_wdi9J0yR_rGoF2ojqz0jjVVQNVI00=)

clean_body: Hello – it’s Eileen Laubacher, the Democrat and retired U.S. Navy Admiral running against Lauren Boebert. Hello – it’s Eileen Laubacher, the Democrat and retired U.S. Navy Admiral running against Lauren Boebert – = I never thought I’d run for Congress. After 34 years in the Navy, I retired planning to spend a lot more time with my husband and our kids. But when I see our constitution and our democracy under attack the way they are right now by people like Lauren Boebert, there was no answer other than to try to serve once again. We can defeat Lauren Boebert and finally end her embarrassing political career. Adam Frisch only lost to Boebert by 546 votes in what was the closest race in the entire country. She is not invincible. And if we do defeat Boebert and flip this seat blue, Democrats are basically guaranteed to take back the House this November! But Lauren Boebert has taken millions of dollars from corporate PACs and the likes of Elon Musk. If we are going to be able to fight back against the Super PAC lies and win, I am counting on grassroots donors like you to chip in to my campaign now. = Please use the links in this email to start a monthly donation through ActBlue: | | = | = | | = | = | | = | = We can finally give Lauren Boebert the boot – but we can only do it together. Thank you, Eileen Rear Admiral Eileen Laubacher (Retired) United States Navy ** Paid for by Eileen for Colorado Eileen for Colorado 9249 S Broadway #200-172 Highlands Ranch, CO 80129 United States Contributions or gifts to Eileen for Colorado are not tax deductible.

name: Julia Letlow
subject: TRUMP-ENDORSED JULIA LETLOW DOMINATES PRIMARY, ADVANCES TO RUNOFF IN FIRST PLACE
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Julia Letlow for Louisiana
body:
Letlow nearly wins outright, defeats Fleming by 17 points, and eliminates Bill Cassidy despite having $15 million spent against her

| | |
---
| | | | [|
---](https://julialetlow.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=70758973fc60553eda6683b43&id=c842e7f201&e=95355a072c)
| | | | | | **FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE** May 17, 2026
**TRUMP-ENDORSED JULIA LETLOW DOMINATES PRIMARY, ADVANCES TO RUNOFF IN FIRST PLACE** _
Letlow nearly wins outright, defeats Fleming by 17 points, and eliminates Bill Cassidy despite having $15 million spent against her_
**BATON ROUGE, LA** — Congresswoman Julia Letlow surged to a commanding first-place finish in Louisiana’s Republican Senate primary last night, earning 45% of the vote and coming within just five points of winning the nomination outright in a crowded Republican primary.
Letlow earned 179,876 votes, decisively defeating John Fleming by 66,448 votes and incumbent Senator Bill Cassidy by 80,397 votes. Fleming finished a distant second with 28%, while Cassidy finished third with 25% and was eliminated from the race.
Letlow did not simply advance to the runoff. She entered it as the clear frontrunner after earning a commanding first-place finish, nearly winning the nomination outright, and building a broad statewide coalition despite nearly $15 million in negative advertising spent against her. Louisiana Republicans saw through the attacks, rejected the noise, and rallied behind President Trump’s endorsed conservative because they are ready for strong leadership that will stand with President Trump, fight for the America First agenda, and put Louisiana first.
_“This campaign has always been about the future of Louisiana and the future of our country,”_**said Congresswoman Julia Letlow.**_“I am so grateful to the people of Louisiana for placing their trust in me, and I am humbled by the strong support we received across our state. President Trump called on me to run for this seat because Louisiana deserves a Senator who will stand with him, fight for our families, and never waver when our values are on the line. Louisiana Republicans sent a clear message that they are ready for new conservative leadership in the United States Senate.”_
Letlow entered the race after receiving President Trump’s complete and total endorsement, making the primary a clear choice between a Trump-endorsed conservative and Senator Bill Cassidy, who voted to convict President Trump.
Fleming now enters the runoff after failing to crack 30% of the vote and finishing 17 points behind Letlow. Saturday’s result showed that while Fleming advanced because someone had to finish second, Letlow built the stronger coalition, earned the broader support, and begins the runoff in the strongest position to finish the job.

---
| | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Paid for by Julia Letlow for Louisiana
---
| | | | | _Copyright © 2026 Julia Letlow for Louisiana, All rights reserved._

Julia Letlow for Louisiana

Baton Rouge, LA 70821

subject: BerlinRosen’s Israel Problem

BerlinRosen built influence with the anti-Israel Left while quietly cashing in on the Jewish and pro-Israel clients its own political machine undermines.

| |
---|---|---
| | | Forwarded this email? [Subscribe here](https://substack..t4QHolp0JqYXTeWs_d7PmhdiFJeCsH7qHoJHusb5Y8Y?) for more
