name: Lily Tang Williams
subject: Going Viral on Facebook and Lily in the News
body:
| | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/lilytangwilliams/going-viral-on-facebook-and-lily-in-the-news?e=c338950e03)
---
| |
---
| |
# Going Viral on Facebook and Lily in the News

**Now going Viral on Facebook with 237K views**

I made this 29- second video on May 11th, my 38th anniversary of coming to America.

[https://www.facebook.com/share/r/1BXW3CWVUs/](https://lilytangwilliams.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=8317033781a524f5f640e7e3e&id=f95ce35ab4&e=c338950e03)

_“38 years ago, I arrived at Austin TX at age 23 with borrowed $100, $1,200 in debt, could not English.
(_1988 arrival at Austin Texas airport)

_Today, I have been married for 36 years with 3 children, run my own home business, live in beautiful New Hampshire.

_(Kids from year 2000)

_USA is the greatest country on earth. No matter where you come from, if you embrace American values and work hard, the sky is the limit.
I am running for Congress in NH as a Republican to keep the American Dream alive.”_

Please donate today $38 to celebrate with me?

[www.lilytangwilliams.com/congress/donate/](https://lilytangwilliams.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=8317033781a524f5f640e7e3e&id=af7eeaf799&e=c338950e03)

**Lily In the News**

1\. Rep. Goodlander Votes ‘Present’ on Pro-Cop Resolution During National Police Week

[Goodlander Votes 'Present' on Pro-Cop Resolution During National Police Week - NH Journal](https://lilytangwilliams.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=8317033781a524f5f640e7e3e&id=198a41b41b&e=c338950e03)

_“Maggie Goodlander refuses to even take a stand on whether she supports the contributions of those who put their lives on the line every day to keep Granite State families safe,”_ Williams said. _“If she can’t take a stand on something so simple and fundamental, what other decisions will she continue to shy away from in Washington?” _

I am Not surprised at all. During 2020 George Floyd riots, as a Groton School alum (class of 2005), she was 1 of the signatories on a letter from alumni demanding that the elite boarding school donate hundreds of thousands of dollars to groups that called for de-funding the police. Granite Staters need to know my opponent's voting record in Congress. She does not represent New Hampshire values in DC. NH District 02 voters deserve better.

2\. NewsNation Interview on Eileen Wang

[China's long-term plan is to soften-up the US from within: Ex-national | Elizabeth Vargas Reports](https://lilytangwilliams.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=8317033781a524f5f640e7e3e&id=b84bb497c2&e=c338950e03)

News Nation had me on to comment on Eileen Wang, a former Mayor of Arcadia CA that was charged as a CCP spy. Her case is a wake-up call. I have been warning Americans for more than a decade about China’s long game plan. We need to reevaluate many of our outdated China policies and counter the CCP's "Soft Power Invasion".

3\. Joe Pags Interview on President Trump’s trip to “Beijing Summit”

[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sk0XbPNGDPU](https://lilytangwilliams.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=8317033781a524f5f640e7e3e&id=baf7e474a6&e=c338950e03)

Mr. Pags and I discussed Xi's REAL goal, why China's economy is collapsing, and where Trump actually has leverage over China. Plus, we discussed Chinese nationals buying land near military bases in New Hampshire, and the 2017 Chinese intelligence law that makes EVERY Chinese passport holder a potential spy.

All Chinese students, business leaders, and even tourists are subject to their National Intelligence Law which mandates them to cooperate/assist CCP for data and information gathering. Many Chinese students belong to pro-CCP United Front Groups like Chinese Students Association.

[Chinese United Front](https://lilytangwilliams.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=8317033781a524f5f640e7e3e&id=400091aa0d&e=c338950e03)

I do not think it is a good idea to allow 500,000 Chinese students here and continue to open up our land for sale to Chinese nationals and companies.

If I am elected, I will be the WATCHMAN in the Congress!

[DONATE](https://lilytangwilliams.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=8317033781a524f5f640e7e3e&id=9e41542d9f&e=c338950e03)

P.S. Don't forget to enjoy the Spring!!
---
| | | | | | | [](https://lilytangwilliams.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=8317033781a524f5f640e7e3e&id=8143a08eef&e=c338950e03)
---
| | [](https://lilytangwilliams.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=8317033781a524f5f640e7e3e&id=9ceb34bfc2&e=c338950e03)

LILY4CONGRESS COMMITTEEPO Box 406Weare, NH 03281

Want to change how you receive these emails?
You can [update your preferences](https://lilytangwilliams.us20.list-manage.com/profile?u=8317033781a524f5f640e7e3e&id=9ff450676b&e=c338950e03&c=3bf22b53df) or [unsubscribe from this list](https://lilytangwilliams.us20.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=8317033781a524f5f640e7e3e&id=9ff450676b&t=b&e=c338950e03&c=3bf22b53df).

I wrote this memo with you in mind. |
Fellow American,

I have to be honest with you.

I am deeply worried about this November and defending our Republican Senate Majority, so I need you to hear me out.

Eight Senate races stand between us and losing the many accomplishments we've worked so hard to build with President Trump.

name: Rob Bonta
subject: Disturbing
disclaimer_text: paid for by American taxpayer dollars
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-26 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-26]
This is disturbing.
On Friday, my office released a new public report with nearly 200 pages of information on the conditions at the seven immigration detention facilities that ICE operates in California — paid for by American taxpayer dollars.
These facilities are where people detained by ICE are held, and what we found during our inspections is alarming and unacceptable.
Since Trump took office again, his administration’s aggressive mass deportation campaign has led to a shocking surge of people in these facilities, which were unprepared for the influx.
We found the facilities to be overcrowded and understaffed, and the conditions are unsafe and unsanitary. They don’t even meet ICE’s own detention standards.
We found evidence of inadequate medical care, including emergency care. We heard countless reports of a lack of basic necessities, issues accessing clean water, and people going hungry unless they were able to purchase extra food at the commissary.
At Adelanto — where the detainee population surged from seven in 2023 to more than 1,500 as of 2025 — we saw murky drinking water coming out of the tap.
At California City, we heard from detainees who experienced extreme cold — using their socks to improvise sleeves and using sheets of paper to block the cold air coming from air vents in their cells.
We heard multiple reported incidents of facilities using pepper spray against detainees, and saw staff who appeared to be using excessive use of force.
Tragically, six people died in these facilities between September 2025 and March 2026.
It’s cruel and inhumane, and it’s far past time for the Trump administration to do something about it.
The people in these facilities are human beings like you and me. They are people who largely have no criminal record. Who, in many instances, had been living in our communities, contributing to our economy, and dutifully checking in with ICE, before suddenly being snatched up and detained. And beyond the basic human rights they are owed, they are afforded civil rights .
There must be oversight and accountability, and I profoundly hope that this report will help bring about the rightful outrage and urgency to improve the conditions of these detention centers and protect the civil rights of the people detained.
Thank you for reading, and for your ongoing support for this important work. It is because of your partnership that I’m proudly able to serve as Attorney General of California.
—Rob
Rob Bonta
Attorney General of California

clean_body: [] This is disturbing. On Friday, my office released a new public report with nearly 200 pages of information on the conditions at the seven immigration detention facilities that ICE operates in California — paid for by American taxpayer dollars. These facilities are where people detained by ICE are held, and what we found during our inspections is alarming and unacceptable. Since Trump took office again, his administration’s aggressive mass deportation campaign has led to a shocking surge of people in these facilities, which were unprepared for the influx. We found the facilities to be overcrowded and understaffed, and the conditions are unsafe and unsanitary. They don’t even meet ICE’s own detention standards. We found evidence of inadequate medical care, including emergency care. We heard countless reports of a lack of basic necessities, issues accessing clean water, and people going hungry unless they were able to purchase extra food at the commissary. At Adelanto — where the detainee population surged from seven in 2023 to more than 1,500 as of 2025 — we saw murky drinking water coming out of the tap. At California City, we heard from detainees who experienced extreme cold — using their socks to improvise sleeves and using sheets of paper to block the cold air coming from air vents in their cells. We heard multiple reported incidents of facilities using pepper spray against detainees, and saw staff who appeared to be using excessive use of force. Tragically, six people died in these facilities between September 2025 and March 2026. It’s cruel and inhumane, and it’s far past time for the Trump administration to do something about it. The people in these facilities are human beings like you and me. They are people who largely have no criminal record. Who, in many instances, had been living in our communities, contributing to our economy, and dutifully checking in with ICE, before suddenly being snatched up and detained. And beyond the basic human rights they are owed, they are afforded civil rights . There must be oversight and accountability, and I profoundly hope that this report will help bring about the rightful outrage and urgency to improve the conditions of these detention centers and protect the civil rights of the people detained. Thank you for reading, and for your ongoing support for this important work. It is because of your partnership that I’m proudly able to serve as Attorney General of California. —Rob Rob Bonta Attorney General of California Before you go, please consider supporting Attorney General Rob Bonta’s work with a contribution of any amount to his re-election campaign. Rob is on the ballot this November, and he needs you on his team to continue his important work as Attorney General. Every donation goes straight to powering our movement for justice and accountability for all. CONTRIBUTE [] Thank you for being a part of this team. CONTRIBUTE NOW [] To contribute via check, please address to: Rob Bonta for Attorney General 2026 c/o Deane & Company 1700 Tribute Road, Suite 201 Sacramento, CA 95815

name: Jon Ossoff
subject: A peek behind the curtain:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jon Ossoff for Senate
body:
# [](https://act.electjon.com/go/4629?t=1&akid=2272%2E5691347%2EsXIUuB)

Peter —
Let me guess: when you open up your email inbox, you're bombarded with emails from political campaigns or advocacy organizations, some of which you've never heard of.
I know it's a lot. And it's why in a sea of promises that "Donald Trump is FINISHED" and breathless "5X MATCH NOW!!!" emails, I work really hard to make sure our messages are different.
So today, I wanted to take a moment to pull back the curtain and explain why we send emails asking you for money and where your donation goes.
In 2021, my campaign raised a record-breaking $162 million _(all thanks to your support!)_ but Republicans spent over $269 million to try to defeat me. I ended up winning my runoff election by just over 1%, an extremely small margin.
I share these numbers with you because the reality is, we have to be prepared for Republicans to spend _even more_ this time around to try to replace me with some henchman who will do Trump's bidding, no questions asked.
Trump and Republicans started this year with more than $600 million cash on hand. They're sitting on a huge war chest that they're ready to spend to maintain their grip on power for years to come. **And I'm their #1 target for defeat.**
Peter, my campaign won't accept contributions from corporate PACs or federal lobbyists. That means the vast majority of the donations that fuel this campaign come from emails just like this one. People chipping in $10, $25, or $50 at a time.
And the money we raise _right now_ means we mobilize more voters in every corner of the state, place more ads to spread my message across Georgia, and hire more staff to sustain and even expand our efforts.
And while I certainly don't expect you or anyone else to donate to every email my team and I send, I hope you'll consider donating to this one.
So here's the part where I ask: [**Can you please make a contribution to my reelection campaign today? When you have people like Trump in power — who act outside of the law — the only solution is absolute victory in the midterm elections.**](https://act.electjon.com/go/32?c0=ossoff-em-may26.KE07xH&refcode=em260517-1-subs&t=2&akid=2272%2E5691347%2EsXIUuB)
_If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly:_
| | **[DONATE $5](https://act.electjon.com/go/34?c0=ossoff-em-may26.hx6_OY&amount=5&refcode=em260517-1-subs&t=3&akid=2272%2E5691347%2EsXIUuB)**
---
****
| **[DONATE OTHER](https://act.electjon.com/go/34?c0=ossoff-em-may26.hx6_OY&refcode=em260517-1-subs&t=4&akid=2272%2E5691347%2EsXIUuB)**
---
****
Thank you. This campaign would be nothing without the support of this team.
Jon
[](https://act.electjon.com/go/32?c0=ossoff-em-may26.KE07xH&refcode=em260517-1-subs&t=5&akid=2272%2E5691347%2EsXIUuB)
---

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Thank you for supporting Jon Ossoff. Email is a critical way to keep up with Jon and his work in the US Senate, but you can click [here](https://act.electjon.com/signup/fewer-emails-signup/?t=6&akid=2272%2E5691347%2EsXIUuB) to sign up to receive fewer emails, or click [here](https://act.electjon.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=7&akid=2272%2E5691347%2EsXIUuB) if you'd like to unsubscribe.

name: State Democrats HQ
subject: Democrats across the country are standing up, speaking out, and fighting back
disclaimer_text: Paid for by State Democrats Committee (StateDemocrats
body:
THIS is what a movement looks like, Peter
#
Peter,
**While Donald Trump and the entire MAGA movement continue running wild, Democrats are standing up, speaking out, and fighting back** – in DC, on the campaign trail, and in communities like yours across the country.
In the House, Democratic Leader **Hakeem Jeffries** is waging an all-out war against Republican attempts to rig the 2026 elections through gerrymandering and voter suppression. And **Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez** is calling out Trump's unauthorized war against Iran for what it is – an unconstitutional power grab with no congressional approval and no clear plan.
In Georgia, Senator **Jon Ossoff** – the only Senate Democrat running for re-election in a state Trump won – is going straight at the president, calling him “spiritually broken” and morally unfit for the office he holds.
**[SUPPORT THE DEMS FIGHTING BACK: CHIP IN](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/104037?refcode=em20260517-10420&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=10420%2E614464%2ECRSl1y)**
**On the campaign trail, the energy is just as fierce.**
In Ohio, **Sherrod Brown** came back from a 2024 loss and immediately went on offense against Trump's tariffs, demanding Ohioans get their money back. And in North Carolina, **Roy Cooper** is running straight at Trump's handpicked candidate – the man Trump personally selected to be RNC chair – and drawing a sharp contrast on the issues voters actually care about.
In Alaska, **Mary Peltola** launched her Senate campaign calling out "the rigged system in D.C. that's shutting down Alaska while politicians feather their own nest.” In Maine, Marine veteran and oyster farmer **Graham Platner** is running an insurgent campaign that has the entire country – Democrats, Republicans, and Independents – taking notice.
Finally, in Texas, **James Talarico** is telling the truth in a way that resonates — not positioning left versus right, but pointing out that the problems our country is facing are more about top versus bottom.
**[SUPPORT THE DEMS FIGHTING BACK: CHIP IN](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/104037?refcode=em20260517-10420&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=10420%2E614464%2ECRSl1y)**
**This is what a movement looks like, Peter.** Democrats are fighting in every state, at every level, against every part of Trump's agenda.
**[Will you help us support all of them this year? clean_body: THIS is what a movement looks like, Peter Peter, While Donald Trump and the entire MAGA movement continue running wild, Democrats are standing up, speaking out, and fighting back – in DC, on the campaign trail, and in communities like yours across the country. In the House, Democratic Leader Hakeem Jeffries is waging an all-out war against Republican attempts to rig the 2026 elections through gerrymandering and voter suppression. And Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez is calling out Trump's unauthorized war against Iran for what it is – an unconstitutional power grab with no congressional approval and no clear plan. In Georgia, Senator Jon Ossoff – the only Senate Democrat running for re-election in a state Trump won – is going straight at the president, calling him “spiritually broken” and morally unfit for the office he holds. On the campaign trail, the energy is just as fierce. In Ohio, Sherrod Brown came back from a 2024 loss and immediately went on offense against Trump's tariffs, demanding Ohioans get their money back. And in North Carolina, Roy Cooper is running straight at Trump's handpicked candidate – the man Trump personally selected to be RNC chair – and drawing a sharp contrast on the issues voters actually care about. In Alaska, Mary Peltola launched her Senate campaign calling out "the rigged system in D.C. that's shutting down Alaska while politicians feather their own nest.” In Maine, Marine veteran and oyster farmer Graham Platner is running an insurgent campaign that has the entire country – Democrats, Republicans, and Independents – taking notice. Finally, in Texas, James Talarico is telling the truth in a way that resonates — not positioning left versus right, but pointing out that the problems our country is facing are more about top versus bottom. This is what a movement looks like, Peter. Democrats are fighting in every state, at every level, against every part of Trump's agenda.
